summary: This notebook demonstrates a hackathon-built multimodal application that leverages the Gemini 3 Pro API to automate product image generation, style/color modification, background removal, and basic market analysis from natural language prompts. It highlights how commercial vision-language models can consolidate traditionally fragmented manual workflows into a single, rapid development pipeline. The author emphasizes the speed and accessibility of building such tools using low-code environments, effectively replacing days of manual editing with minutes of API-driven automation. Readers should take away a practical example of integrating multimodal LLMs for early-stage product design and marketing tasks without training custom models.
competition_flow: A user provides a text prompt or product image, which is processed by the Gemini 3 Pro API to generate multi-angle visuals, apply stylistic changes, remove backgrounds, and output structured market insights, with the final output saved as a submission file.
insights: Commercial multimodal APIs can effectively replace multi-step manual pipelines for product visualization and basic competitive analysis.
Low-code development environments significantly reduce the time required to prototype and deploy functional AI applications.
